boundary_reason: The card presents a strong integrated architecture idea, but its confidence claim still outruns the mostly compositional and cross-link-based evidence style.
classification_rationale: The overvaluation signal dominates because proposal-level confidence is already high while the evidence remains synthesis-heavy and non-operational.
why_not_likely_overvaluation: Not applicable here because this case is the likely-overvaluation anchor: the overvaluation pressure already dominates the defensibility signal.
why_not_justified_exception: There is no bounded operational defect, no repeated validation and no code-level grounding that would justify treating the confidence spike as an exception.
why_not_adjacent_classification: This is not a justified exception because there are no concrete code references, no repeated bounded validation and no narrow operational surface. It is stronger than a borderline case because the proposal remains synthesis-heavy while carrying proof-like confidence pressure.
bounded_reading: Read as a promising synthesis that likely wants softer confidence if touched later, not as settled proof and not as a reason to open S2 semantics.

source_snapshot.overvaluation_signal: proposal confidence is already at 90 while the card is still cross-link and integration heavy
source_snapshot.defensibility_signal: the proposal does show coherent architectural integration rather than pure noise
source_snapshot.review_anchor: Existing proposed_high_confidence review treats this cluster as the clearest likely-overvaluation pressure point.
